symptoms.label: Weld snaps or cracks during strike test
symptoms.description: When a test weld between two scrap pieces is struck with a hammer, the weld is brittle and snaps or cracks rather than deforming.

causes.label: Insufficient heat / lack of fusion into base metal
Contaminated base metal (rust, paint, oil, mill scale)
Wrong filler rod or electrode for the base material
Inadequate shielding gas or flux coverage causing porosity
Poor welding technique (travel speed, torch angle, arc length)

checks.question: Was the base metal cleaned to bare bright metal before welding?
checks.positive_meaning: User confirms base metal was cleaned prior to welding
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Rule out contamination; investigate heat input and technique.

checks.question: Take a photo of the broken weld cross-section.
checks.positive_meaning: Photo of the fracture surface for evaluation of porosity, fusion depth, and color

inadequate_shielding.note: Visible porosity/pinholes indicates poor gas coverage

insufficient_heat_lack_of_fusion.note: Shallow penetration visible on fracture surface

checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Inspect fracture surface for porosity (gas issue) or shiny unfused interface (heat issue).

checks.question: Is the filler rod/electrode rated for the base metal you are welding?
checks.positive_meaning: Filler is correctly matched to base metal
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Filler is likely not the issue; focus on heat and technique.

checks.question: Is the amperage setting within the recommended range for the material thickness?
checks.positive_meaning: User confirms amperage matches recommended range for thickness
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Heat input is likely adequate; investigate technique or contamination.

checks.question: Did you maintain a steady travel speed and consistent arc length/torch angle?
checks.positive_meaning: Technique was consistent
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Technique is likely not the issue; check heat and material prep.

symptoms.label: Excess penetration or burn-through
symptoms.description: Weld droops on top and underneath, or falls through entirely, making a hole.

causes.label: Excessive material at weld (wire feed too high)

checks.question: Has the weld fallen through entirely, leaving a hole?
checks.positive_meaning: Severe burn-through, indicating excessive heat input
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Reduce wire feed speed and weld current, increase travel speed

checks.question: Are you moving the gun slowly or pausing in one spot during the weld?
checks.positive_meaning: Travel speed is too slow, depositing too much heat per inch
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Increase travel speed and keep it steady

checks.question: Is the wire feed speed set near the upper end of the recommended range?
checks.positive_meaning: Too much filler material being deposited
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Reduce wire feed speed

checks.question: Is the workpiece thin (e.g., sheet metal under 1/8")?
checks.positive_meaning: Thin material overheats easily
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Reduce weld current and use shorter weld passes

symptoms.label: Inadequate penetration
symptoms.description: Weld does not penetrate the joint fully, just sits on the surface.

causes.label: Incorrect welding technique (CTWD, arc position, gun angle)
Workpieces too thick or gap too tight
Insufficient weld material (wire feed too low)

checks.question: Is your contact-tip-to-work distance (CTWD) more than 1/2"?
checks.positive_meaning: Excessive stickout reduces current and penetration
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Maintain 1/2" or less CTWD

checks.question: Are you aiming the arc behind (trailing edge of) the weld puddle?
checks.positive_meaning: Arc not focused on leading edge, reducing penetration
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Keep arc on leading edge of weld puddle

checks.question: Are you moving the gun quickly across the joint?
checks.positive_meaning: Insufficient dwell time to develop penetration
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Reduce travel speed and/or increase weld current

checks.question: Are the workpieces thick (>1/4") and joined without a bevel?
checks.positive_meaning: Joint geometry prevents the arc from reaching the root
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Bevel workpieces, allow slight gap, weld in multiple passes

checks.question: Is the wire feed speed set near the lower end of the recommended range?
checks.positive_meaning: Not enough filler deposited
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Increase wire feed speed

symptoms.label: Bend at joint
symptoms.description: Workpieces have warped or bent at the welded joint.

checks.question: Were the workpieces firmly clamped and tack-welded before the main weld?
checks.positive_meaning: Workpieces were restrained against distortion
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Clamping appears adequate; investigate heat instead

checks.question: Did you weld a long continuous bead without pausing to let it cool?
checks.positive_meaning: Heat accumulated and distorted the joint
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Weld in small portions, allow cooling, increase travel speed, reduce wire feed

symptoms.label: Weld not adhering properly
symptoms.description: Gaps present between weld and previous bead or between weld and workpiece.

causes.label: Incorrect welding technique (bead placement, gun angle, arc position)
Insufficient weld material (wire feed too low)

checks.question: Is there visible rust, paint, mill scale, or oil on the workpiece surface where you welded?
checks.positive_meaning: Surface contamination preventing fusion
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Clean workpiece down to bare metal

checks.question: Did you place the stringer bead away from the root of the joint?
checks.positive_meaning: Bead not located where fusion is needed
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Place stringer bead at correct place in joint; pause at sides during weave

checks.question: Is the welder current set near the low end of the recommended range for this material thickness?
checks.positive_meaning: Not enough heat to fuse the base metal
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Increase current and wire feed speed

checks.question: Is the groove or gap between workpieces very narrow (arc can't reach the bottom)?
checks.positive_meaning: Joint geometry prevents proper fill and adhesion
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Widen groove or increase bevel

checks.question: Does the weld bead look noticeably thin or undersized?
checks.positive_meaning: Insufficient filler deposited
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Increase wire feed speed

symptoms.label: Wire Weld - Porosity
symptoms.description: Small cavities or holes in the weld bead, visible from the top.

checks.question: Is the polarity set correctly for the type of wire/welding you are using?
checks.positive_meaning: Polarity is correctly set for the welding process

incorrect_polarity.note: Correct polarity rules out the polarity cause

checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Polarity is fine; investigate other causes.

checks.question: Is the shielding gas flow rate below 15 CFH (or otherwise low)?
checks.positive_meaning: Shielding gas flow is insufficient to protect the weld pool
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Increase gas flow, clean nozzle, and maintain proper CTWD.

checks.question: Is the shielding gas you are using the type recommended by the wire supplier?
checks.positive_meaning: Correct gas mix is being used for the wire
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Gas type is correct; investigate other causes.

checks.question: Is the workpiece or welding wire visibly dirty, rusty, oily, or coated (e.g., paint, mill scale)?
checks.positive_meaning: Contamination is present on the base metal or wire
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Clean workpiece to bare metal and ensure wire is clean and free of oil/coatings.

checks.question: Did your travel speed vary noticeably during the weld (start/stop, jerky motion)?
checks.positive_meaning: Travel speed was not steady
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Use two hands or rest on a steady surface and maintain consistent travel speed.

checks.question: Is the contact-tip-to-work distance greater than about 3/4 inch (19 mm)?
checks.positive_meaning: Stickout is excessive

insufficient_shielding_gas.note: Long CTWD also degrades gas coverage

checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Reduce CTWD to roughly 3/8–3/4 in.

symptoms.label: Wire Weld - Excessive Spatter
symptoms.description: Spatter around the weld bead that is grainy and large (fine spatter is normal).

checks.question: Is the workpiece or welding wire visibly dirty, rusty, oily, or coated?
checks.positive_meaning: Contamination is present on the base metal or wire
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Clean workpiece to bare metal; ensure wire is clean.

checks.question: Is the polarity set correctly for the type of wire you are using?
checks.positive_meaning: Polarity matches process requirements
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Polarity is fine; investigate other causes.

checks.question: Is the shielding gas flow rate low (below ~15 CFH)?
checks.positive_meaning: Inadequate gas coverage
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Increase gas flow, clean nozzle, maintain proper CTWD.

checks.question: Does the wire appear to be feeding faster than the puddle can absorb (stubbing into the work or pushing the gun back)?
checks.positive_meaning: Wire feed speed is too high for the current
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Reduce wire feed speed.

checks.question: Is the contact-tip-to-work distance greater than about 3/4 inch?
checks.positive_meaning: Stickout is excessive
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Reduce CTWD.

symptoms.label: Wire Weld - Crooked/Wavy Bead
symptoms.description: The weld bead is not straight; it wanders or has a wavy path.

checks.question: Were you welding freehand without bracing your hand or arm on a steady surface?
checks.positive_meaning: Operator did not stabilize the gun
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Use two hands or rest your hand on a steady surface.

checks.question: Did your travel speed vary noticeably during the weld?
checks.positive_meaning: Travel speed not steady
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Maintain steady travel speed.

checks.question: Is the contact-tip-to-work distance greater than about 3/4 inch?
checks.positive_meaning: Stickout is excessive, reducing arc control
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Reduce CTWD.

symptoms.label: Wire Weld - Burn-Through
symptoms.description: Base material melts away, leaving a hole in the weld.

causes.label: Excessive material at weld (too much wire deposited)

checks.question: Are the current setting and/or wire feed speed set higher than recommended for this material thickness?
checks.positive_meaning: Heat input is too high for the base material
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Reduce current and/or wire feed speed.

checks.question: Were you moving the gun slowly, lingering in one spot?
checks.positive_meaning: Excess heat is dumped into a small area
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Increase travel speed and keep it steady.

checks.question: Is the bead unusually tall or wide compared to the joint, suggesting too much wire deposited?
checks.positive_meaning: Too much filler being added
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Reduce wire feed speed.

checks.question: Is the base material thin sheet (e.g., under ~1/8 inch / 3 mm)?
checks.positive_meaning: Thin material is more prone to burn-through
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Reduce current/WFS and increase travel speed; consider stitch welding.

symptoms.label: Inadequate stick weld penetration
symptoms.description: Stick weld bead sits on top of the workpiece with shallow or no fusion into the base metal

causes.label: Welding current too low
causes.prior_note: Manual lists 'Increase current' as first correction

causes.label: Travel speed too fast (welding too quickly)
causes.prior_note: Manual lists 'Weld more slowly' as second correction

causes.label: Arc length too long, dispersing heat

checks.question: After cleaning slag, does the bead sit narrow and raised on the surface with little fusion into the base metal?
checks.positive_meaning: Visual signature of inadequate penetration (cold weld)
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Increase current and/or weld more slowly

checks.question: Were you moving the electrode quickly along the joint?
checks.positive_meaning: Travel speed too fast reduces heat input to workpiece
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Weld more slowly

checks.question: Is the amperage setting at or below the low end of the recommended range for the electrode and material thickness?
checks.positive_meaning: Current is insufficient for proper penetration
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Increase current

manual_citation.section: Stick Weld Diagnosis – Workpiece Heat Control / Weld Penetration

symptoms.label: Excess penetration or burn-through
symptoms.description: Stick weld penetrates too deeply or burns a hole through the workpiece

causes.label: Welding current too high
causes.prior_note: Manual lists 'Decrease current' as first correction

causes.label: Travel speed too slow (dwelling on the joint)
causes.prior_note: Manual lists 'Weld more quickly' as second correction

causes.label: Workpiece too thin for chosen settings

checks.question: Is there a hole burned through the workpiece or a deep crater on the back side?
checks.positive_meaning: Clear evidence of excess heat input / burn-through
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Decrease current and/or weld more quickly

checks.question: After cleaning slag, is the bead unusually wide, irregular, or undercut at the edges?
checks.positive_meaning: Visual signature of current too high
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Decrease current

checks.question: Were you moving the electrode slowly or pausing along the joint?
checks.positive_meaning: Slow travel increases heat input and penetration
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Weld more quickly

checks.question: Is the amperage setting at or above the high end of the recommended range for the electrode and material thickness?
checks.positive_meaning: Current is excessive for the workpiece
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Decrease current

manual_citation.section: Stick Weld Diagnosis – Workpiece Heat Control / Weld Penetration

symptoms.label: Stick weld bead has abnormal appearance
symptoms.description: After cleaning slag, the bead does not look like a proper, evenly rippled weld (too narrow, too wide, ropy, or irregular)

causes.label: Current too low (narrow, ropy bead with poor fusion)
Current too high (wide, flat bead, possible undercut/spatter)
Weld speed too fast (narrow, irregular bead)
Weld speed too slow (wide piled-up bead)
Arc length too short (electrode sticks, ropy bead)
Arc length too long (wide, spattery bead with poor fusion)

checks.question: Is the cleaned bead narrow, tall, or ropy (piled up) with poor fusion to the base metal?
checks.positive_meaning: Signature of low current or fast travel
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Increase current or weld more slowly

checks.question: Is the cleaned bead wide and flat, possibly with undercut at the edges or heavy spatter?
checks.positive_meaning: Signature of high current, slow travel, or long arc
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Decrease current, weld faster, or shorten arc

checks.question: Does the electrode tend to stick to the workpiece when you start or weld?
checks.positive_meaning: Arc length too short or current too low
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Increase distance between electrode tip and workpiece (lengthen arc)

checks.question: Is there heavy spatter and an erratic, hard-to-control arc?
checks.positive_meaning: Arc length too long
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Decrease distance between electrode tip and workpiece (shorten arc)

checks.question: Did you remove the slag coating before inspecting the bead?
checks.positive_meaning: Confirms inspection is valid; slag obscures the true bead appearance
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Proceed with bead diagnosis; if no, clean slag first per 'CLEAN WELDS FIRST!' note

symptoms.label: Stick weld excess penetration or burn-through
symptoms.description: Weld droops on top and underneath, or falls through entirely making a hole

checks.question: Is the welding current set above the recommended range for your electrode/material thickness?
checks.positive_meaning: Current setting is too high for the workpiece, causing overheating
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Reduce current setting

checks.question: Are you moving the electrode slowly or pausing/lingering over the joint?
checks.positive_meaning: Travel speed is too slow or inconsistent, depositing too much heat
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Increase welding speed and keep it steady

checks.question: Has the weld fallen through the workpiece creating a hole?
checks.positive_meaning: Burn-through occurred — confirms excess penetration symptom
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Reduce current and/or increase travel speed

manual_citation.section: Stick Weld Penetration (Workpiece Heat Control) — Excess Penetration or Burn-Through

symptoms.label: Stick weld inadequate penetration
symptoms.description: Weld does not contact the joint fully, sitting just on the surface

causes.label: Incorrect welding technique (arc not on leading edge / wrong torch angle)
Insufficient weld heat (current too low / speed too fast)
Workpieces too thick or gap too small

checks.question: Is the welding current set below the recommended range for your electrode/material thickness?
checks.positive_meaning: Current is too low to melt into joint
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Increase current and slow down so fill material has time to melt in

checks.question: Are you moving the electrode quickly across the joint?
checks.positive_meaning: Travel speed too fast — not enough heat input
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Slow down so fill material has time to melt into the weld location

checks.question: Are you keeping the arc on the trailing edge or middle of the puddle rather than the leading edge?
checks.positive_meaning: Arc placement is not directing heat into unmelted base metal
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Keep arc on leading edge of weld puddle and hold torch at proper angles

checks.question: Are the workpieces thick (>1/4 in) and joined without a bevel?
checks.positive_meaning: Joint geometry prevents weld from reaching the root
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Bevel thick workpieces, allow slight gap, and weld in several passes

checks.question: Does the weld bead appear thin or under-filled compared to the joint?
checks.positive_meaning: Not enough filler being deposited
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Increase amount of fill material

symptoms.label: Stick weld not adhering properly
symptoms.description: Gaps present between weld and previous bead or between weld and workpiece

checks.question: Is the workpiece surface coated with paint, rust, mill scale, oil, or other contaminants?
checks.positive_meaning: Surface contamination is preventing fusion
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Clean workpiece down to bare metal

checks.question: Is the welding current set below the recommended range?
checks.positive_meaning: Not enough heat to fuse weld to base metal
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Increase current

checks.question: Is there a visible gap between the two workpieces larger than the electrode diameter?
checks.positive_meaning: Joint gap is too wide to bridge
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Decrease distance and increase bevel

checks.question: Is the stringer bead placed off-center or at the wrong location in the joint?
checks.positive_meaning: Bead placement or torch angle preventing fusion to bottom of joint
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Place stringer bead correctly, adjust angle, keep arc on leading edge

checks.question: Does the weld bead look thin or starved of filler?
checks.positive_meaning: Not enough fill material deposited
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Increase amount of fill material

manual_citation.section: Stick Weld – Weld Not Adhering Properly

symptoms.label: Stick weld bend at joint
symptoms.description: Workpieces have bent or distorted at the welded joint

checks.question: Were the workpieces firmly clamped and/or tack welded before welding?
checks.positive_meaning: Workpieces were restrained — clamping is unlikely to be the cause
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Investigate heat input as primary cause

checks.question: Did you weld a long continuous pass without letting the workpiece cool?
checks.positive_meaning: Heat input was concentrated and prolonged, causing thermal distortion
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Weld a small portion and allow to cool before proceeding; increase weld speed

checks.question: Did you make tack welds at multiple points to hold workpieces before the main weld?
checks.positive_meaning: Tack welds were used to constrain the joint
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Clamping is unlikely the cause; look at heat input

symptoms.label: Stick Weld - Porosity
symptoms.description: Small cavities or holes in the stick weld bead.

causes.label: Dirty workpiece or fill material
causes.prior_note: Listed first in manual's possible causes

causes.label: Inconsistent welding speed
causes.prior_note: Listed second in manual's possible causes

checks.question: Is the workpiece or electrode visibly contaminated with oil, rust, paint, or coatings?
checks.positive_meaning: Surface contamination is present that can outgas during welding and cause porosity

dirty_workpiece_or_fill_material.note: Manual identifies dirty workpiece/fill as a direct cause

checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Clean workpiece down to bare metal; ensure fill and electrode are free from oil, coatings, and residues.

checks.question: Did your travel speed vary noticeably during the weld?
checks.positive_meaning: Travel speed was not steady, allowing variation in arc time and gas coverage
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Maintain a steady weld speed; use two hands or rest hand on a steady surface.

symptoms.label: Stick Weld - Crooked/Wavy Bead
symptoms.description: Stick weld bead is crooked or wavy rather than straight.

checks.question: Were you welding freehand without bracing or resting your hand on a steady surface?
checks.positive_meaning: Hand was unsupported, leading to wavering torch position
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Use two hands or rest hand on a steady surface.

checks.question: Did your travel speed vary during the weld pass?
checks.positive_meaning: Travel speed varied, causing inconsistent bead width and shape
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Maintain a steady weld speed.

symptoms.label: Stick Weld - Excessive Spatter
symptoms.description: Spatter around the bead is grainy and large rather than fine. Fine spatter is normal.

causes.label: Dirty workpiece or fill material
causes.prior_note: Manual lists this as the only cause

checks.question: Is the spatter grainy and large rather than fine?
checks.positive_meaning: Spatter pattern is abnormal and indicates a problem

dirty_workpiece_or_fill_material.note: Manual: 'Fine spatter is normal. Spatter that is grainy and large is a problem.'

checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Clean workpiece to bare metal; ensure fill material and electrode are clean and free from oil, coatings, and residues.

checks.question: Is the workpiece or electrode visibly contaminated with oil, rust, paint, or coatings?
checks.positive_meaning: Surface contamination present that disrupts arc stability and increases spatter
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Clean workpiece down to bare metal; clean fill material and electrode.

symptoms.label: Stick Weld - Burn-Through
symptoms.description: Base material melts away, leaving a hole in the weld.

checks.question: Is the welder's current setting high relative to the base metal thickness?
checks.positive_meaning: Amperage is high enough to overheat thin base metal
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Reduce welding current.

checks.question: Were you moving the electrode slowly along the joint?
checks.positive_meaning: Heat input per unit length is high due to slow travel
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Increase welding speed and keep it steady.

checks.question: Did you deposit a large amount of fill material at the burn-through location?
checks.positive_meaning: Excess filler concentrated heat and material in one spot
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Reduce amount of fill material.

symptoms.label: Wire Feed Motor Runs but Wire Does Not Feed Properly
symptoms.description: The wire feed motor is running and audible, but the welding wire is not being fed out of the gun properly.

causes.label: Damaged MIG Gun, cable, or liner assembly

checks.question: Does the groove size stamped on the feed roller match the wire diameter you are using?
checks.positive_meaning: Feed roller is the correct size for the wire
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Rule out incorrect roller size; investigate tension and gun/liner.

checks.question: Is the feed tensioner knob set very tight (high pressure setting)?
checks.positive_meaning: Tensioner is over-tightened, potentially deforming or jamming the wire
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Loosen Feed Tensioner so it applies only enough pressure to prevent continued spinning after the Gun Trigger is released.

checks.question: When the motor runs, does the wire slip under the feed roller (roller spins but wire barely moves)?
checks.positive_meaning: Feed roller is not gripping wire — pressure too low
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Increase wire feed pressure properly per step 27 on page 17.

checks.question: Is the MIG gun, cable, or liner visibly kinked, cut, or damaged?
checks.positive_meaning: Mechanical damage in the gun/cable/liner path
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Have a qualified technician inspect and replace as necessary.

symptoms.label: Wire Creates a Bird's Nest During Operation
symptoms.description: Wire bunches up into a tangled mass ('bird's nest') at the feed mechanism during welding.

causes.label: MIG Gun Cable Connector not fully inserted into Wire Feed mechanism

checks.question: Is the contact tip stamped with a size matching your wire diameter?
checks.positive_meaning: Contact tip is correctly sized for wire
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Rule out incorrect tip; investigate pressure and gun connector.

checks.question: Is the MIG Gun Cable Connector fully inserted into the wire feed mechanism with no gap visible?
checks.positive_meaning: Connector is properly seated
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Rule out connector issue; check pressure and contact tip.

checks.question: Is the feed tensioner set to a high pressure value?
checks.positive_meaning: Excess wire feed pressure likely
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Adjust wire feed pressure properly per step 27 on page 17.

symptoms.label: Wire Stops During Welding
symptoms.description: Wire feeding starts but then stops or intermittently halts during a weld.

causes.label: Gun cable severely bent; feed mechanism cannot feed wire
Gun liner too small for welding wire being used
Wire is tangled on the spool
Wire is not making contact with Feed Rollers
Feed Roller is not making enough contact with wire, or is crushing flux-cored wire

checks.question: Is the gun cable severely bent, looped tightly, or kinked?
checks.positive_meaning: Cable geometry is restricting wire feed
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Straighten the gun cable and retry.

checks.question: When you look at the wire spool, is the wire cross-wound, overlapping, or tangled?
checks.positive_meaning: Spool is tangled and binding
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Replace or rewind the spool; check tension on the spool hub.

checks.question: Is the wire seated in the feed roller groove between the inlet and outlet guides?
checks.positive_meaning: Wire is properly engaged with the feed rollers
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Rule out feed-roller contact issue; investigate liner and cable.

checks.question: If using flux-cored wire, is the wire emerging from the feed rollers visibly flattened or crushed?
checks.positive_meaning: Feed roller pressure is crushing the flux-cored wire
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Loosen tension and/or verify knurled roller appropriate for flux-cored wire.

checks.question: Is the gun liner rated for the diameter of wire you are using?
checks.positive_meaning: Liner is correctly sized for wire
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Rule out liner sizing; inspect liner for clog/wear.

symptoms.label: Welding Arc Not Stable
symptoms.description: The welding arc sputters, flickers, or is otherwise unstable during operation.

causes.label: Incorrect Contact Tip or liner size, or excessive wear
Loose MIG Gun cable or ground cable
Damaged MIG Gun or loose connection within Gun
Incorrect polarity for process being run
Gas coverage insufficient or too high
Loose electrode cable or ground cable
Damaged electrode holder or loose connection inside it
Current setting incorrect for the job

checks.question: Is the machine set to DCEP for MIG, or DCEN for Flux-Cored self-shielded welding?
checks.positive_meaning: Polarity is correct for the selected process
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Rule out polarity; check other causes.

checks.question: Is the ground clamp firmly attached to clean, bare metal on the workpiece?
checks.positive_meaning: Workpiece connection is solid
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Rule out ground connection; investigate other causes.

checks.question: Are all gun and ground cable connections at the machine firmly tightened (no wobble)?
checks.positive_meaning: External cable connections are tight
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Rule out loose cables; investigate other causes.

checks.question: Is the contact tip visibly worn, elongated, or burned at the opening?
checks.positive_meaning: Contact tip is worn out
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Replace contact tip with proper size for wire used.

checks.question: Is the shielding gas flow rate set according to the Settings Chart?
checks.positive_meaning: Gas flow is within recommended range
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Rule out gas flow; verify gun cable connector seating with no O-Rings exposed.

checks.question: Is the wire feeding smoothly and consistently out of the gun?
checks.positive_meaning: Wire feed itself is not the source of instability
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Rule out wire feeding; check polarity, gas, and ground.

checks.question: Are all electrode and ground cable connections tight?
checks.positive_meaning: Cables are tight, making loose connections less likely
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Inspect electrode holder and current setting next.

checks.question: Is the electrode holder visibly damaged, cracked, or feel loose?
checks.positive_meaning: Damaged or loose electrode holder is likely causing the unstable arc
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Have a qualified technician inspect and repair/replace as necessary.

checks.question: Does your current setting match the recommended setting on the chart for your electrode and material?
checks.positive_meaning: Current setting matches recommendation, ruling out incorrect setting
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Move on to other possible causes.

checks.question: Is the shielding gas cylinder pressure low or near empty?
checks.positive_meaning: Low gas supply could be destabilizing the arc
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Replace the shielding gas cylinder.

symptoms.label: Weak Arc Strength
symptoms.description: The welding arc is weak or underpowered, producing poor penetration or weak fusion.

causes.label: Improper gauge or length of cord (e.g., extension cord)
Not enough current (welder set too low for metal thickness)
Improper gauge or length of power cord

checks.question: Are you powering the welder through an extension cord (rather than the supplied power cord directly into a wall outlet)?
checks.positive_meaning: An extension cord is in use, which the manual prohibits
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Do not use an extension cord on this welder. Use only the supplied power cord or an identical replacement.

checks.question: Is the measured line voltage at the outlet within the welder's specified range?
checks.positive_meaning: Line voltage is acceptable
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Rule out line voltage; check current setting and cord.

checks.question: Is the current/heat setting set appropriately for the thickness of metal being welded?
checks.positive_meaning: Current setting is appropriate for the workpiece
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Rule out current setting; verify line voltage and cord.

checks.question: What is the measured line voltage at the outlet (in volts)?
checks.positive_meaning: Voltage below specification confirms incorrect line voltage
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Have a licensed electrician remedy the line voltage situation.

symptoms.label: Welder does not function when switched on
symptoms.description: Welder is switched on but does not operate; may show a warning screen on LCD.

causes.label: Circuit supplies insufficient input voltage or amperage
Machine is in low- or over-voltage protection
Machine is in the incorrect mode
Ground clamp not attached to workpiece

checks.question: Is a warning screen appearing on the LCD display?
checks.positive_meaning: Welder has detected overheating or a voltage fault
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: If overheat warning, wait with Power Switch ON for unit to cool; if voltage warning, verify input voltage and press Reset Button on back of machine.

checks.question: Had the welder been running for an extended period before it stopped?
checks.positive_meaning: Duty cycle may have been exceeded leading to thermal shutdown
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Allow welder to cool; reduce duration/frequency of welding periods (see Duty Cycle on page 19).

checks.question: Is the supply circuit input voltage within the specified range on the Specifications table?
checks.positive_meaning: Input voltage is within spec; insufficient supply is unlikely
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Rule out supply issues; investigate other causes.

checks.question: Is the gun/trigger connection properly seated on the machine?
checks.positive_meaning: Trigger connection is good
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Rule out trigger; check other causes. If not seated, reseat or have qualified technician replace trigger.

checks.question: Is the correct welding process selected on the machine?
checks.positive_meaning: Mode selection is correct
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Rule out mode error; investigate other causes.

checks.question: Have you been welding for an extended period or at high duty cycle just before the failure?
checks.positive_meaning: Heat buildup likely tripped the thermal protection device

tripped_thermal_protection.note: Thermal trips correlate strongly with extended use beyond duty cycle

checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Let the welder cool down; reduce duration/frequency of welding per Duty Cycle on page 29.

checks.question: Is the ground clamp securely attached to the workpiece?
checks.positive_meaning: Ground clamp is properly attached, ruling out that cause
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Move on to inspect other possible causes.

checks.question: Is the shielding gas hose connected to the welder?
checks.positive_meaning: Shielding gas is connected, ruling out that cause
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Move on to other possible causes.

checks.question: Does the trigger appear loose, damaged, or improperly connected?
checks.positive_meaning: Trigger connection issue is likely the cause
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Have a qualified technician check and secure/replace the trigger.

manual_citation.section: Troubleshooting - Welder Does Not Function When Switched On

symptoms.label: LCD display does not light when welder is switched on
symptoms.description: When the welder is switched on, the LCD display does not illuminate.

causes.label: Unit is not connected to outlet properly
Plug does not have correct rating
Circuit breaker on welder has tripped due to high input amperage
Input power cord is not seated properly

checks.question: Is voltage present at the outlet when measured?
checks.positive_meaning: Outlet is powered
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Outlet is OK; check connection, plug, and welder breaker.

checks.question: Is the circuit breaker or GFCI for this outlet tripped?
checks.positive_meaning: Outlet supply is interrupted at the breaker/GFCI
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Determine and remedy cause before resetting the breaker/GFCI.

checks.question: Is the installed plug the correct rating per the Specifications on page 7?
checks.positive_meaning: Plug rating matches specification
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Plug is correct; rule out this cause.

checks.question: Is the circuit breaker / Reset Button on the back of the welder tripped?
checks.positive_meaning: Welder's onboard breaker has tripped
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Press Reset Button on back of machine to reset the circuit breaker.

checks.question: Is the twist-lock input power cord fully secured at the welder?
checks.positive_meaning: Cord is properly seated
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Cord seating is OK; investigate other causes.

checks.question: Is the welder's power cord fully plugged into the outlet?
checks.positive_meaning: Plug is fully seated, making improper connection less likely
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Verify outlet voltage next.

checks.question: Do other devices plugged into the same outlet receive power?
checks.positive_meaning: Outlet is powered, ruling out an unpowered outlet
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Focus on the welder's power cord connection and internal protection.

checks.question: Is the circuit breaker or GFCI for this outlet tripped?
checks.positive_meaning: Tripped breaker confirms outlet is unpowered
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Determine and remedy the cause before resetting; verify circuit is rated for welder amperage (see Specifications, p. 7).

manual_citation.section: Troubleshooting - LCD Display Does Not Light When Welder is Switched On

symptoms.label: Wire feeds, but arc does not ignite
symptoms.description: Welding wire feeds out of the gun, but no arc is established when attempting to weld.

checks.question: Is the ground clamp attached to clean bare metal near the welding location?
checks.positive_meaning: Ground connection is good
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Ground is OK; inspect contact tip.

checks.question: Is the contact tip the correct size for the welding wire being used?
checks.positive_meaning: Contact tip size matches wire
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Tip size is correct; inspect for wear or fouling.

checks.question: Is the hole in the contact tip deformed or enlarged?
checks.positive_meaning: Contact tip is worn out
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Replace contact tip with proper size and type.

checks.question: Does the contact tip appear dirty, fouled, or carry spatter/residue?
checks.positive_meaning: Contact tip is contaminated
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Clean the contact tip properly.

manual_citation.section: Troubleshooting - Wire Feeds, but Arc Does Not Ignite

symptoms.label: Porosity in the weld metal
symptoms.description: Visible pores, holes, or pinholes in the finished weld bead.

causes.label: Not enough or too much shielding gas
Gun is being used too far away from workpiece (excessive CTWD)
Polarity is incorrect for the application

checks.question: Does the shielding gas bottle have pressure remaining on the gauge?
checks.positive_meaning: Bottle is not empty
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Bottle not empty; check regulator and flow.

checks.question: Is the gas regulator showing proper shielding gas flow rate for the process?
checks.positive_meaning: Gas flow is within proper range
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Gas flow OK; inspect workpiece, technique, polarity, and wire.

checks.question: Is the workpiece cleaned down to bare metal at the weld area?
checks.positive_meaning: Workpiece surface is clean
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Workpiece OK; check other contamination sources.

checks.question: Is the contact-tip-to-work distance (CTWD) longer than recommended?
checks.positive_meaning: Gun is being held too far from workpiece
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Hold gun closer per recommended CTWD procedure.

checks.question: Is the polarity set to DCEP for MIG (or DCEN for Flux-Cored) as appropriate?
checks.positive_meaning: Polarity matches the process
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Polarity correct; inspect other causes.

checks.question: Does the welding wire show rust, oxidation, or residue?
checks.positive_meaning: Wire is contaminated and may introduce porosity
checks.recommended_action_if_positive: Replace with clean wire free of rust and residues.

manual_citation.section: Troubleshooting - Porosity in the Weld Metal
